content: Start the concept note interview for Porto Alegre. Use the /concept-note skill flow. Always use the ask_user tool for multiple-choice questions instead of writing them as text.
Good — city profile loaded. Now let me ask the core Phase 1 questions all at once:
Waiting for your answers to the 4 questions above — click the options in the UI and I'll continue building the concept note right away! 🌿

Continue the concept note interview from Phase 1. Here is what has been filled so far:
proponent: municipio: Porto Alegre; estado: Rio Grande do Sul; populacao: ~1.388.794 habitantes (est. 2025); região metropolitana ~4.269.000

Pick up where we left off — ask the next questions needed.
Good — proponent basics are locked in. Now let's complete Phase 1. I need three more things from you: **sector**, **objective type**, and **project identity**.

Three questions are now on your screen — please select your answers for all three and I'll immediately fill in the project identity and suggest a project name. 

A few things I already know from the Porto Alegre context that will help once you answer:
- **NBS + Adaptation** is the most BPJP-aligned combination given the May 2024 floods context
- **SMAMUS** is the natural proponent since it hosts the climate action plan (PLAC) and manages environmental licensing
- Once sector + objective type are confirmed, I'll suggest a project name and draft the `project_id` section automatically
🌿 Soluções Baseadas na Natureza (NBS); 🔄 Ambos — co-benefícios de adaptação e mitigação; SMAMUS – Sec. Municipal do Meio Ambiente, Urbanismo e Sustentabilidade
Perfect choices — NBS + Both + SMAMUS is exactly the combination that resonates with BPJP/C40 funders given Porto Alegre's post-2024 flood context. Let me lock these in.
Now, based on Porto Alegre's context (May 2024 floods, Arroio Dilúvio, lowland vulnerability, SMAMUS leadership), here are three project name options:

**Why these three options?**
- Option 1 — broadest, gives flexibility to combine interventions (flood parks + bioswales + urban forest) and directly references the 2024 disaster, which is a strong funder hook
- Option 2 — the Arroio Dilúvio is Porto Alegre's most visible urban waterway problem (heavily channelized, crosses the city center along Av. Ipiranga); naming the corridor signals a clear territorial boundary
- Option 3 — leans into carbon sequestration and the city's existing urban forest strength (1 million trees, top-15 most forested city in Brazil)

Pick one, or type your own — I'll save it and move straight into **Phase 2: Context & Diagnosis**.

**"Super Porto Alegre"** — bold, memorable, and carries a strong recovery + transformation message. Saved. ✅

Moving to **Phase 2: Context & Diagnosis**. I have the city's climate data pre-loaded, so let me auto-draft the territorial context and put the key decisions to you.
Good — context and diagnosis auto-filled from the knowledge base. Now the two key decisions for Phase 2:

Two questions on screen. A note on each:

- **Territorial scope**: The Guaíba lowlands option is the strongest funder narrative (48% of the city flooded, clear before/after story), but the Dilúvio corridor has very high urban density and visual impact. Multiple basins is the most ambitious but needs stronger governance capacity to explain.

- **Climate risks**: You can select multiple — most NBS projects address 2–3 risks simultaneously, which strengthens the co-benefits case for funders.
